# Update to the global critical habitat screening layer

The process for compiling the complete Critical Habitat layers comprises three main steps, split across seven separate scripts.

## 1 Data preprocessing

### 1.1 Red_List_Preprocessing.R

Queries the IUCN Red List to produce four input datasets for the analysis: CR, EN, VU species, and Great Apes. The script includes code, currently commented out, that can add a desired buffer on these data.

### 1.2 Data_Preprocessing_Raster.R

Preprocesses any input data only available in raster format. This script inclues the processing of extremely high resolution tropical moist forest and mangrove data, both of which can take days to complete.

### 1.3 Data_Preprocessing_Vector.R

The main preprocessing script: preprocesses all vector input data.

## 2 Creating Critical Habitat layers

### 2.1 Create_Drill_Down_Critical_Habitat_Raster_Layer_WGS.R

Combines all standardised input data from 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3 to produce the finished drill down raster layer with accompanying attribute table in WGS 84.

### 2.2 Create_Drill_Down_Critical_Habitat_Raster_Layer_Mollweide.R

As previous, but outputs in Mollweide equal-area.

## 3 Post-processing

### 3.1 Create_Basic_Critical_Habitat_Raster_Layers.R

"Flattens" the drill down raster layers produced in 2.1 and 2.2 to only contain one attribute: whether a cell is likely Critical Habitat (10), potential (1), or unclassified (0).

### 3.2 Create_Drill_Down_Polygons.R

Polygonises the drill down raster layers produced in 2.1 and 2.2 and reorders the variables to be more user-friendly in a way not possible with raster data.
